---
title: "Queue-Reactive LOB Simulator"
subtitle: "A limit order book simulation framework based on the Queue-Reactive model for large-tick assets"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}

::: {.hero-content}

The QR simulator reproduces realistic order book dynamics by sampling events — limit orders, cancellations, trades — from empirical distributions conditioned on the current book state. A C++ engine handles the simulation loop; a Python pipeline estimates parameters from [Databento](https://databento.com) market data.

::: {.hero-badges}
[C++20]{.badge} [CMake]{.badge} [Python 3.11+]{.badge} [Apache Arrow]{.badge}
:::

:::

:::

## Documentation

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.doc-card}
### [Model and Data](model.qmd)
The QR framework, data sources, estimation pipeline, and the probability biasing mechanism for market impact.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Order Book](orderbook.qmd)
Book representation, event types, order processing transitions, and the C++ `std::map`-based implementation.
:::

::: {.doc-card}
### [Simulation Loop](simulation.qmd)
How to configure, assemble, and run a QR simulation — from a barebones loop to bias injection and output recording.
:::

:::

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone --recurse-submodules https://github.com/SaadSouilmi/Queue-Reactive.git
cd Queue-Reactive
git submodule update --init --recursive
podman build -t qr .
podman run --rm -it qr bash
```

## Project Layout

```
qr/
├── cpp/
│   ├── include/        # Headers (orderbook, qr_model, simulation)
│   ├── src/            # Implementation
│   └── bin/            # Executables (sample, sample_strategy, run_metaorder, ...)
├── src/qr/             # Python estimation & analysis package
├── scripts/            # Plotting scripts
├── notebooks/          # Jupyter notebooks (paper figures, calibration)
├── configs/            # JSON simulation configs
├── tests/              # C++ unit tests (GoogleTest)
└── data/               # Input data & results
```
